title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government's response to the 2020 report addresses issues related to artificial intelligence and public standards. The response outlines the government's plans and policies for regulating and developing AI in accordance with public standards.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document is a report that assesses the impact of copyright on the use of copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems. It explores how copyright laws apply to AI-generated content and the potential implications for creators and developers.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy on generative AI outlines its stance on the use of artificial intelligence in educational settings. The policy provides guidance on how generative AI can be employed to support teaching and learning in a responsible and effective manner.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan is an initiative to harness the benefits of artificial intelligence (AI) in the transport sector, while also mitigating potential risks. The Department for Transport (DfT) will be working with the sector to explore and implement AI technology.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report discusses how copyrighted works are being used in the development of artificial intelligence systems, and it presents updates on this usage as required by law. It likely aims to provide clarity on the balance between innovation through AI and copyright protections.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is creating a policy to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in medical devices to ensure they are safe and effective. This policy is a response to recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council to better oversee AI in medical settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's policy on regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications, which has been requested by the education and science secretaries. It provides Ofqual's approach to this regulation, but specifics are not provided in the title alone.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government is responding to the House of Lords Select Committee's recommendations on artificial intelligence, aiming to address potential risks and benefits of AI in the country. This response outlines the government's plan to work with various stakeholders and implement new policies to ensure the safe and responsible development of AI in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a review into how artificial intelligence affects public standards in various areas. The review aims to establish guidelines and recommendations for the integration of AI into these public areas.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL has made a submission to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on how to effectively govern artificial intelligence. This submission provides written evidence to help inform the committee's findings on the governance of AI.
